summary: The reproduction launches a minimal TUS app with `max_size(1024)` and verifies that a deferred-length chunked upload still stores 1536 bytes and reports `Upload-Offset: 1536`.
expected_security_impact: The vulnerable TUS handler should advertise `Tus-Max-Size: 1024` yet still accept and store a deferred-length chunked upload larger than 1024 bytes.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned the pinned `v0.89.3` source tree, built the helper image, launched the probe TUS app, observed `Tus-Max-Size: 1024`, and still stored a 1536-byte deferred-length chunked upload with `Upload-Offset: 1536`.
verification_method: The exploit first checks `OPTIONS /files` for `Tus-Max-Size: 1024`, then creates a deferred-length upload, streams a 1536-byte chunked PATCH without `Content-Length`, and confirms both `Upload-Offset: 1536` and an on-disk file size of 1536 bytes.
evidence_produced_by_run: The exploit printed `OPTIONS Tus-Max-Size: 1024`, confirming the configured guard value exposed by the vulnerable handler.
The exploit printed `PATCH status: 204`, `PATCH Upload-Offset: 1536`, and `HEAD Upload-Offset: 1536`, confirming the oversized upload was accepted.
The exploit printed `Stored file size: 1536`, confirming data was persisted beyond the configured 1024-byte cap.
The container reported `[evohunt] tus max-size verification passed` and `SUCCESS: reproduction completed and verified`.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
